source: RCA-14 / SAO-1/5 super-admin PII export readiness inventory
decision: CEO-approved safest existing tier: PII report exports require super_admin; UUID-only report exports keep the support floor.
remainingOpsFollowUp: Notify lower-tier exporters that PII report types now return 403 unless the operator is super_admin, and review export audit logs after deploy.

artifacts.readinessRole: Runtime export gate: four PII report types require super_admin, two UUID-only types remain support, and type is validated before auth/data access.
Pins REG-198 so PII report types cannot drift below super_admin or move the gate after data access.
evidence: gates the 4 PII types (students/teachers/parents/audit) at super_admin
keeps the 2 UUID-only types (quizzes/chats) at the support floor
returns the unknown-type 400 BEFORE calling authorizeAdmin

artifacts.readinessRole: CSV egress defense-in-depth: message text and context JSON are redacted before observability export leaves the system.
Pins REG-202 egress redaction for free-form message text, context JSON, and redactor behavior.
evidence: Tier-2 PR B — super-admin export message redaction (P13) — REG-202
wraps row.message in redactPIIInText(...).text before it reaches the CSV
redacts an email in a free-form message
redacts an Indian mobile number in a free-form message

artifacts.readinessRole: Exports the shared PII redaction utilities to Next.js routes without duplicating the Deno shared implementation.
Tracks the remaining non-code readiness action: notify lower-tier staff and confirm no legitimate non-super-admin PII export workflow depends on the previous access level.
evidence: non-super-admin staff exporting these 4 types get HTTP 403 on deploy

artifacts.readinessRole: Turns the remaining RCA-14 ops follow-up into an executable notification and audit-review runbook for lower-tier exporters after PII report tiering.
